title: foundation — health aggregator contract + base probes
description: Foundation OWNS the health substrate. `HealthAggregator` is the container-bound service every module's service provider registers probes with. `/api/health` walks every registered probe and returns aggregate status. This file (a) defines the probe interface every downstream module implements and (b) enumerates the base probes foundation itself ships.

public.description: Aggregate only. No probe-detail leaked. Cached 5s to avoid stampedes.

platformAdmin.shape: { status, checks: [{ name, module, category, critical, status, message, duration_ms, last_success_at, last_failure_at, consecutive_failures }] }
platformAdmin.description: Full probe output. Requires platform.health.view permission.

kubernetesLiveness.description: Ultra-lightweight; only runs probes with category=`liveness`. Called by kubelet.

kubernetesReadiness.shape: same as /api/health but filtered to category=readiness
kubernetesReadiness.description: Called by kubelet on readiness probes. A failing readiness probe pulls the pod from load balancing but does not restart it.

probeCategories.liveness: The application is up and can serve traffic AT ALL. Failure → restart the container.
probeCategories.readiness: The application is READY to serve traffic (downstream deps reachable, migrations run). Failure → remove from load balancer but keep running.
probeCategories.startup: One-time boot check. Only evaluated once at container start. Failure → the container is misconfigured and cannot run.

aggregateStatusRules.degraded: At least one non-critical probe failing; every critical probe passing.
aggregateStatusRules.unhealthy: At least one critical probe failing.

probes.name: app-booted
probes.description: The service-provider chain completed. HealthAggregator sets this on ApplicationBooted event; probe returns cached result. Fails at boot only.

probes.name: db-connectivity
probes.description: Runs `SELECT 1` against the primary database connection. Fails when connectivity is lost or the pool is exhausted.

probes.name: db-read-replica-lag
probes.description: Read-replica lag from the primary. Warns > 5s, fails > 30s. Non-critical because we can degrade to primary reads.

probes.name: cache-connectivity
probes.description: Redis PING + SET/GET round-trip on a canary key. Fails when Redis is unreachable or evictions are hot.

probes.name: queue-connectivity
probes.description: Horizon dashboard's supervisors list is reachable + at least one worker is running. Fails when the queue backend is dead.

probes.name: queue-depth-under-threshold
probes.description: Warns when any queue's depth exceeds its per-queue threshold (default 10000). Non-critical because a backed-up queue does not stop the app.

probes.name: search-engine-reachable
probes.description: Pings the configured Scout engine (Meilisearch by default). Non-critical — search degrades gracefully to a filtered DB query when the engine is unavailable.

probes.name: module-registry-loaded
probes.description: ModuleRegistry booted every discovered module + resolved the dependency graph without cycles. Fails at boot only.

probes.name: config-cache-fresh
probes.description: The compiled config cache (`bootstrap/cache/config.php`) is present + its mtime is within 24h of the current deploy timestamp. Warns on staleness — production deploys should always ship a fresh cache.

downstreamRegistration.howToRegister: Every module's service provider registers its probes in `boot()` via `$this->app->make(HealthAggregator::class)->probe(new MyProbe())`. Probes are collected in dependency order + walked in registration order on aggregate calls.
conventions: Probes MUST be idempotent and cheap. Anything > 1s should be moved to a background snapshot job + the probe reads the snapshot.
Probes MUST NOT throw. Timeouts + errors are captured by the aggregator and reported as `failing` with the message.
